changeSummary: Added a contributor-agnostic completeness repair admission gate in `context/lib/ai/agentic-execution.ts` that only accepts same-tool repairs as a spent semantic retry when resolve-first or probe-first recovery materially changes discovery or target-resolution behavior.

When the verifier asks for same-tool retargeting but a patch only adds guards or otherwise fails the deterministic retargeting check, the shared runtime now rejects that patch and immediately escalates to the existing bounded replan path within the same retry attempt.

Added focused unit coverage in `context/tests/unit/agentic-execution-heuristics.test.ts` for both the rejection case (guard-only same-tool patch) and the acceptance case (discovery-expanded repair with explicit target resolution).

notes: The new gate keys off generic completeness signals such as probe-first or resolve-first recovery, changed discovery behavior, and named-target resolution diagnostics rather than contributor names, venue names, tool allowlists, or benchmark strings.
The admission logic only evaluates shared runtime code shape and shared policy diagnostics, so it remains reusable for any contributor exposing discovery, lookup, list, search, or detail methods.
Route wrappers were not edited; parity remains anchored in the shared execution engine under `context/lib/ai/agentic-execution.ts`.

SDK smoke query `Show me the most active sports markets on Polymarket today.` against `http://localhost:3000` completed successfully with `outcomeType: "answer"`, non-empty output, `status: "completed"`, and no completeness repair events.

SDK smoke query `Compare the current implied odds for Spain, Brazil, and France in the 2026 FIFA World Cup winner market on Polymarket.` against `http://localhost:3000` completed successfully with `outcomeType: "answer"`, non-empty output, `status: "completed"`, and no completeness repair events.
Code-path parity held because both `context/app/api/v1/query/route.ts` and `context/app/(chat)/api/chat/route.ts` still import `executeWithSelfHealing` from `@/lib/ai/agentic-execution`.
No browser chat QA was run; `chatPassed` reflects shared-runtime parity rather than automated live chat interaction.

revertInstructions: If the orchestrator rejects this experiment, restore `/Users/alex/Documents/context-sdk/examples/server/polymarket-contributor/validation/runtime-experiment-snapshot.latest.json` with `context/scripts/runtime-research-experiment-state.ts` before rerunning any validation.

After restoration, rerun `pnpm exec tsx --import ./tests/unit/server-only-shim.ts --test ./tests/unit/agentic-execution-heuristics.test.ts`, `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`, `pnpm exec biome check "lib/ai/agentic-execution.ts" "tests/unit/agentic-execution-heuristics.test.ts"`, and the two SDK smoke queries against `http://localhost:3000`.

finalDecision.reason: Rejected after deterministic max-iterations decision: execution did not improve, codeSafety dropped by 0.22, and the loop exhausted its maximum iteration budget.

openRisks: Repository-wide typecheck is still red from pre-existing `context/lib/ai/scout-orchestration.ts` errors about `enableExactTargetExternalProbe`, so focused validation cannot be marked fully green even though the touched-area tests passed.

The targeted `biome check` remains red from pre-existing style and performance debt already present in `context/lib/ai/agentic-execution.ts`, plus file-formatting drift in the existing test file; IDE lints for the edited files were otherwise clean.

The retargeting gate currently requires concrete evidence such as added discovery steps, added explicit target-resolution signals, or clearing the shared named-target-resolution diagnostic. Orchestrator remeasurement is still needed to confirm that this heuristic admits the right repairs and shrinks the `repair_without_full_recovery` cluster without over-rejecting legitimate same-tool fixes.
